checks.a_analysis: The source PDF begins with a title slide (Page 1) containing the text 'GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL SPRING 2025 QUARTERLY MEETING'. In the rendered HTML output, this primary title information is entirely absent from the start of the document. The output begins instead with the content from slide 2 ('Check In and Get Ready!'). Furthermore, the logical flow is disrupted as the 'Council Members' section (from slide 4) is placed before the 'Who We Are' section (from slide 5). These omissions and rearrangements significantly change the visual organization and initial context of the document.
checks.b_evidence_source: GEOGRAPHIC INFORMATION COORDINATING COUNCIL SPRING 2025 QUARTERLY MEETING
checks.b_evidence_output: [Text is missing; document starts with 'Check In and Get Ready!']

checks.a_analysis: The source document does not contain traditional grid-based tables. It uses bulleted lists and grouped text blocks to present member information (e.g., Pages 4, 20, 25). These elements have been converted into standard HTML lists, which maintain the readability and organization of the data. No broken table structures were observed.

The source PDF contains dozens of meaningful and essential images, including committee logos (Page 11), panel/meeting photos (Pages 3, 7, 15, 19, 24), disaster imagery (Pages 29, 36), and critical map/dashboard screenshots (Pages 30, 31, 32, 33, 40, 44, 45, 52, 55). In the HTML output, nearly all of these meaningful images are missing. Many are replaced by non-functional green placeholder blocks, while others, such as the suite of agency logos on slide 11, are omitted entirely with no placeholder. Only the QR codes and the main conference logo appear to have rendered correctly.
checks.b_evidence_source: Agency logos (Page 11), Disaster Response photos (Page 29), GIS Dashboard screenshots (Pages 30-32)
checks.b_evidence_output: [Images replaced by green blocks or entirely absent]
checks.b_evidence_location: Throughout document, specifically sections corresponding to Pages 11, 29, and 30-32

checks.a_analysis: A thorough visual scan of the rendered HTML output shows that content blocks (paragraphs, lists, headings) appear only once in the body of the document. There are no instances where body content from the slides is repeated unnecessarily.
